coverage: 100% of reachable node types across the 296-group, 2,413-node closure, including its six-object pipe structure collection.

material.residual: Eevee and Three.js/PMREM retain different high-frequency procedural filtering, bump derivatives, environment sampling, and Principled BRDF response.

validation: The authored six-way pipe layout, generated sleeves, SDF joint shell, dual-mesh smoothing, auto-smooth, structure toggle, and bone preview run in-browser. The nested tight-wrap group now evaluates in Blender's active-object world translation and returns to browser-local space immediately after auto-smooth, leaving other joined components unchanged. Both 25 x 24 x 23 Volume Cube branches have zero sign disagreements with Blender; their Volume to Mesh outputs are exact at 3,496 / 3,494 and 3,308 / 3,306. Dual Mesh preserves Blender's cyclic polygon start on all 3,496 isolated reference faces, so downstream quad tessellation and the 127 selected 30-degree edges agree. Installed Blender 5.1.2 and GN-VM both produce 11,163 / 8,064 at the authored default, 10,857 / 7,644 at joint size 18, 6,195 / 3,306 with structure hidden, and 31,447 / 28,386 in bone preview. Its 3,306 blue filament faces and 4,758 mahogany faces now reproduce Blender's evaluated material ownership and missing-attribute black fallback. The aligned authored captures reach 0.9914 silhouette IoU, complete mutual one-pixel coverage, 0.0489 macro luminance MAE, and +0.00331 mean-luminance error. The filament surface is a viewport shader; the supplied project contains no slicer toolpath or source G-code. The archived Blender 4.3 reference stores one fewer split vertex at the authored default and hidden-structure cases but identical faces and four-decimal local bounds; this is documented as version variance rather than hidden with an angle bias.
